other websites and their front-end designs so Awards is a great place to look if you want to see how pros are actually creating frontends and you don't want to see the 10 millionth version of another boring SAS website now it's at this point we need to talk very quickly about the actual purpose of this exercise because despite the click-baity title the purpose isn't just to steal $100,000 websites with claude code you are not creating moon you are not a rooftop bar in Rome and this goes beyond just templating it right because obviously you would template this and then put your own spin on it the point is twofold one it's to expose you to different types of websites right most people just see the same exact stuff over and over and over again so going to places like awards will let you see like what's actually out there creatively secondly and more importantly the process of trying to clone these websites requires you to figure out how they even put it together in the first place do you understand what's actually going on in the scrolling animation whatsoever probably not yet today you're going to start to see okay like oh here's how they did that here's how they did this and in the process of copying them you are going to gain a ton of front-end design knowledge which is huge because that's how you build any skill in any domain right you don't become good at something by just trying it in a vacuum and think you're going to become awesome absolutely not you see what the pros do you try to copy them you fail you copy them again you get a little bit better then you add your own twist and then over time after very very very very very very many repetitions then you can create something truly original and unique this is just part of the process so that's step one find a website you like something that you can imagine templating and then putting your own spin on and that brings us to step two which is the site tearown this is where we're actually going to figure out what the heck is going on in this website right how are they doing this parallax how are they doing the shading what's going on in the scroll animation and this is where cloud code is going to come in as well so cloud code is going to need three things from us in order to figure out what the heck is going on under the hood it's going to need the HTML it's going to need the CSS and it's going to need the JavaScript the HTML is essentially the bones of the web page okay how things are structured the CSS think of it as like the clothes of the website the styling and then JavaScript is how things actually interact with each other right if you press a button what happens that's kind of JavaScript right that's very reductive but essentially think of those as the three building blocks of any front-end web page now the first thing we need to give cloud code is the HTML and is actually completely available to us so if I go on this web page what I'm going to do is I'm going to hit control-U that's going to bring up all the HTML then I'm going to select all i'm going to copy it and then I will paste all of that HTML into Cloud Code okay that's the first step and then I'm going to provide some screenshots so for this I would take a screenshot of the main page i would take a screenshot of it at the end here on the second bit and then I would probably take some screenshots of this whole shadowing effect so it understands what's going on but that is only two out of the three things we need because we also needed to figure out the CSS and the JavaScript but luckily for us Cloud Code can grab those on its own so here I am looking at the HTML again at the very end at the very end what do we see down here we see files for the JavaScript and also the CSS so once we give Claude Code the plugin we're going to say "Hey I want you to take a look and figure out what's going on in the JavaScript and CSS files to do that Claude Code is going to use web fetch." And I didn't understand this at first but claude code when it uses web fetch this session of cloud code isn't actually fetching anything directly when you use web fetch on cloud code it's actually calling a smaller model to do it on its behalf if you look at the documentation it's kind of vague as to like what's actually happening but the point of that is these files can be kind of big so if you just hand it the HTML and say "Hey cloud code go look at the JS go look at the JavaScript file go look at the CSS file." It's not going to bring back everything so what I use instead is I have a specific site tearown skill that essentially just has an enhanced prompt saying "Hey look at everything in JavaScript look at everything in CSS." And pull back all the relevant information now I will have a copy of that skill inside the free Chase AI community there's a link to that in the description to recap what do we need in step two to get the site tearown we need the HTML which we paste we need to give it the URL we need to give it screenshots plus we need to use the site tearown skill so it can grab the JavaScript and the CSS that way instead to create this is we will create a video and then we will have Claude code pull out the frames of the video so we will create an image with the moon looking like this and then create an image with the moon looking like this create a video of the transition pull the frames and then we'll mimic this that's what we did here this all came from a video now for our asset creation we are going to be using Nanobanana Pro and VO3.1 and I'm using both of these inside of Higsfield which is my one-stop shop for any and all AI content creation tools so we're just going to go to image go to Nano Banana Pro and we are going to create the starting image and ending image for our moon video as it transitions from fully illuminated to fully shadowed and importantly we're going to do it on a green screen now why are we doing it on a green screen well obviously the way this is working this isn't like a background video you can see this moon is like its own thing right it's its own individual asset so we can't have some sort of background you know following it as it goes from the hero to this next page and that isn't as simple as just turning it into a PNG because remember it's going to be a full-blown video that's going to have 50 60 70 frames or whatever and AI is going to have to pull out the moon from the background so doing a green screen actually makes that pretty easy for AI and to do that is very simple you just take a screenshot right of the web page i have the moon completely shadowed and then I do one with it completely illuminated throw it into here and I give it a very basic prompt that says remove all text and make the background a green screen using Nano Banana Pro that's it now this process also applies for any other assets right like this cocktail glass right so have it create an image of that also put it on a green screen next we need to turn this into a video so I went up to video went to Google Vo 3.1 and you can see some of my creations here so we have
